compositional_truth_check.p3_pothos: Present (Black Pot with White Rabbit anchor)

inventory_reconciliation.notes: P2 and P4 are effectively non-functional. P3 shows severe senescence. P1 remains the only stable specimen.

plant_audit.p2: Dead/Absent. Soil surface shows no active growth.
plant_audit.p3: Severe chlorosis and necrosis. The primary leaf is desiccated and drooping.
plant_audit.p4: Dead/Absent. No evidence of the 'Silver Guest' specimen.

biome_observations.surface_debris: White rabbit anchor remains in P3. No fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows P3 with a large, yellowing leaf and a smaller green sprout.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Progressive necrosis of the large P3 leaf; the smaller sprout in P3 remains static.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Total collapse of P3 foliage; P2 and P4 remain barren throughout the sequence.

vision_report.anomalies: The 'Silver Guest' (P4) and 'Mexican Mint' (P2) are not visible in any frame, suggesting they were either never established or lost prior to the start of this sequence.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is in a state of advanced decline. P3, the anchor pot, is suffering from severe physiological stress, likely due to substrate desiccation or root failure, evidenced by the rapid browning and collapse of the primary leaf. P1 is the only specimen maintaining structural integrity. P2 and P4 are essentially empty, indicating a systemic failure of the intended biome registry.
